active_phase: Phase 2 - First public release and post-release hardening
current_task: tighten the public narrative around v0.1.1 and prepare the first low-noise discovery push from a now-clearer GitHub landing page
next_focus: make the first problem-first public post, likely in r/sideprojects
watch whether the public README and first launch copy make the app's value obvious to strangers
collect real feedback on the public shortcut picker
watch for whether users need a broader preset list or smarter conflict suggestions
keep the bundled-runtime installer as the preferred download path
keep the new hotkey picker preset-based and simple unless real user demand proves a more complex editor is worth it
continue clean-machine validation and deeper packaged-launch trust work
keep the older portable PyInstaller build secondary until its earlier trust question is explicitly revisited and cleared
reuse the retrospective lessons as the default release and cleanup baseline for the next small Manna utility
last_request: /build manna-snips can we add more discriptiion for the public eyes as to what the software does on the github page? and how can we advertise or share this some more?. i was thinking a reddit post or something similar just to get some basic traction
